customizer — multi-asset refactor: change src/types/customization.ts so logoPlacement becomes an assets array. Each CanvasAsset has: id, type ('logo' | 'text'), previewUrl (for logos), text/font/color (for texts), x, y, width, rotation, zoneId, view ('front' | 'back'). Update customizerStore.ts with addAsset/updateAsset/removeAsset/clearAssets actions. Keep backward compat: expose logoPlacement getter that returns the first 'logo' asset for legacy code that reads it. Max 5 files changed.

customizer — AssetsPanel: create src/components/customizer/AssetsPanel.tsx showing a list of all canvas assets (logos + texts) with thumbnail, zone label, and a delete button (X). Clicking an asset selects it on the canvas. Empty state: 'Aucun élément ajouté encore'. Bilingual. Slot it into the right side of ProductCustomizer (desktop only, stack below on mobile).

customizer — text asset feature: in ProductCustomizer step 2 (or a new sub-step), add a 'Ajouter du texte' button next to the logo uploader. Modal asks: text, font (3 choices: Inter bold, Georgia italic, Impact), color (navy/white/gold/red/black), size. Creates a 'text' asset and shows it on the canvas. Render text assets in ProductCanvas.tsx as fabric.Text objects.

customizer — canvas delete on select: in ProductCanvas.tsx, when an asset is selected, show a small circular X button above it. Clicking removes the asset via store. Use fabric.js's controls API or overlay a DOM button positioned from the selected object's coordinates. Test on both logos and texts.

customizer — right sidebar layout: restructure ProductCustomizer so on desktop (md:) there's a permanent right sidebar (~280px wide) containing the AssetsPanel + a mini 'Upload logo' button + 'Add text' button, so users don't have to go back to step 2 to add more. Steps become orchestration for first-time flow but panel is always accessible.

customizer — center everything: audit ProductCustomizer modal layout. Canvas should be truly centered in its container (no left/right bias). Step content should be centered vertically on screen. Modal should be centered with max-width. Check both mobile and desktop.

colors — audit COLOUR_HEX_MAP in src/lib/shopify.ts vs actual Shopify variant color names. Add any missing names. Fix hex values that look visually wrong (e.g. a 'Forest Green' that's actually dark olive). Keep compatible with findColorImage fallback logic.

product descriptions — audit src/data/products.ts descriptions if any exist, or wherever description text shows on ProductDetail.tsx. Rewrite to be MORE GENERAL — not technical fabric jargon, just: 'Un hoodie unisex confortable, prêt pour ton logo' rather than '80% coton / 20% polyester ringspun...'. 3 paragraphs max per product type (not per SKU).

product card — hide SKU everywhere it's shown to customers. Use categoryLabel(category, lang) from src/lib/productLabels.ts instead. Search for local.sku in src/components/ and src/pages/ (excluding admin). Keep SKU in data attributes (data-sku='...') for internal identification.
admin — add /admin/quotes page: list all quotes with status pills, client, total, discount, date, view count. Drawer with quote details. Mock data.
admin — add /admin/login page: clean email+password form, 'Forgot password?' link, Supabase auth-ready placeholder. Route at /admin/login. Not yet wired to real auth.
vendor — add src/pages/vendor/VendorLayout.tsx similar to AdminLayout but with vendor-specific nav (Dashboard, My Quotes, New Quote, Settings). Route /vendor/*.
vendor — VendorDashboard: widgets for quotes sent (7d), conversion rate, commission earned, quick 'new quote' button, recent activity feed. Premium UI.
vendor — QuoteList at /vendor/quotes: table of all quotes this vendor sent, status pill, client, total, age. Quick actions (duplicate, view link, resend).

client-facing — QuoteAccept page at /quote/:id: shows vendor-built cart (products, colors, sizes, placement, pricing), 'Upload your logo' dropzone, T&C checkbox, 'Review & Pay' button (stub). Mobile-first premium UI. Bilingual based on URL ?lang= or browser lang.

client-facing — create src/components/LogoUploadDropzone.tsx: drag-drop, preview, file validation (png/svg/pdf/ai, max 20MB), remove button, bilingual labels. Reusable between customizer and QuoteAccept.
visual polish — hero revamp: tighter typography, bigger headline, clearer value prop about 5 jours ouvrables delivery. Test 375px + 1440px viewports.
visual polish — Boutique grid: premium product card, better price display, fast-delivery badge on all products, improved filter UI on mobile
visual polish — CartDrawer: cleaner line item layout, clearer edit/remove actions, better empty state with CTA to boutique
new feature — DeliveryBadge component: reusable '5 jours ouvrables' badge with bolt icon + subtle gold accent. Use on hero, product cards, cart.
new feature — TrustSignalsBar: thin strip below hero with 4 signals (5 jours ouvrables delivery / Made in Quebec / Quality guarantee / 500+ companies). Icons from lucide, bilingual.
new feature — StepsTimeline: a premium horizontal timeline on homepage: Day 1 Conception → Day 2 Production → Day 3 Delivery. Icon-driven, clean animations.

mobile polish — ensure all fixed bottom elements (BottomNav, CartDrawer, modal CTAs) respect safe-area-inset-bottom. Test with grep for 'fixed bottom' and ensure each has env(safe-area-inset-bottom) padding

a11y deep pass — src/pages/Index.tsx and src/components/customizer/*: aria-hidden on decorative icons, aria-label on interactive icon-only buttons, proper heading order (h1 → h2 → h3 without skipping), focus-visible rings on all interactive, reduced-motion guard on marquee and major animations
performance — add loading='lazy' + width/height to all below the fold, wrap heavy product lists in React.memo, audit for unnecessary re-renders
SEO — index.html: add JSON-LD Organization + Product schemas, ensure canonical URL, og:image, twitter:card, bilingual meta description that matches current lang
error handling — wrap /admin, /vendor, /quote routes in route-level error boundaries with friendly fallback UIs
loading states — all admin pages should show skeleton loaders when data fetches (using mock Promise.resolve for now to establish the UI pattern)
print-ready — on the client QuoteAccept page, add a 'Print quote PDF' button (UI only, wiring later). Premium print stylesheet for the quote layout
polish — footer revamp: 4-column layout, newsletter signup, social icons, bilingual, copyright. Works in admin/vendor/public layouts.
